items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Ivy Global/Ivy Global SAT 2016.pdf
items.prompt:
At the West site, after a garden replaced part of its lawn with native plants, observed pollinators rose by midsummer. The change occurred during week 10 of the first full month and was based on 69 observations.

Which inference is best supported by the text?
items.correctChoice: The native plants may have attracted additional pollinators.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Ivy Global/Ivy Global SAT 2016.pdf
items.prompt: A student claims that a tutoring program involving 26 participants had a measurable effect during a fall trial. Which finding, if true, would most directly support the claim?
items.correctChoice: After students attended at least six sessions, their average quiz score rose by 12 points.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Ivy Global/Ivy Global SAT 2016.pdf
items.prompt:
During the field update, after reviewing 36 field notes, the researcher's summary was unusually ______: it was supported by independent records and repeated observations.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word?
items.correctChoice: credible

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Ivy Global/Ivy Global SAT 2016.pdf
items.prompt:
During a May design review with 22 planners, the text first describes a challenge associated with electric buses, especially charging costs. It then explains a possible benefit: maintenance savings.

Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
items.correctChoice: It presents a drawback of an approach and then identifies a potential advantage of that approach.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Ivy Global/Ivy Global SAT 2016.pdf
items.prompt:
In a follow-up test after 38 days, researchers predicted that seeds would germinate quickly in warmer soil. ______ the warmest samples produced the fewest seedlings.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
items.correctChoice: However,

items.prompt:
A 2011 study of battery design surveyed 319 participants and compared 118 sites that differed in storage capacity. After tracking the sites for 6 months, the researchers found that a revised separator allowed cells to hold a charge for longer periods. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of battery design found that differences in storage capacity were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Kaplan /Kaplan5.pdf
items.prompt:
At the West site, after a school posted assignment checklists online, late homework submissions decreased. The change occurred during week 10 of the first full month and was based on 83 observations.

Which inference is best supported by the text?
items.correctChoice: The checklists may have helped students track deadlines.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Kaplan /Kaplan5.pdf
items.prompt: A student claims that a composting project involving 315 participants had a measurable effect during a fall trial. Which finding, if true, would most directly support the claim?
items.correctChoice: After cafeteria waste was sorted daily, trash volume fell by about one-third.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Kaplan /Kaplan5.pdf
items.prompt:
During the lab meeting, after reviewing 45 field notes, the researcher's summary was unusually ______: it focused on steps that could be completed with the available budget.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word?
items.correctChoice: pragmatic

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Kaplan /Kaplan5.pdf
items.prompt:
During a May planning session with 16 planners, the text first describes a challenge associated with online archives, especially scanning expenses. It then explains a possible benefit: wider public access.

Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
items.correctChoice: It presents a drawback of an approach and then identifies a potential advantage of that approach.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Kaplan /Kaplan5.pdf
items.prompt:
In the next round of observations after 21 days, the museum planned to display the sculpture outdoors. ______ conservation staff warned that rain could damage its surface.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
items.correctChoice: However,

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Kaplan /Kaplan5.pdf
items.prompt:
By June, the biology class had finished week 58 of the lab notebook after reviewing 35 records ______ by September, the lab partners had begun testing the results.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

items.prompt:
A 2014 study of battery design surveyed 331 participants and compared 82 sites that differed in storage capacity. items.prompt:
A 2014 study of bird migration surveyed 476 participants and compared 67 sites that differed in seasonal timing. After tracking the sites for 3 months, the researchers found that tracking data showed that some birds adjusted routes after unusually warm springs. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of bird migration found that differences in seasonal timing were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

After tracking the sites for 3 months, the researchers found that simplified station labels helped riders plan transfers more quickly. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of public transit maps found that differences in wayfinding were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.prompt:
A 2012 study of public transit maps surveyed 838 participants and compared 66 sites that differed in wayfinding. After tracking the sites for 10 months, the researchers found that simplified station labels helped riders plan transfers more quickly. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of public transit maps found that differences in wayfinding were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2017 05 VN.pdf
items.prompt:
At the East site, after a library extended its evening hours, attendance at student study groups doubled. The change occurred during week 8 of the first full month and was based on 97 observations.

Which inference is best supported by the text?
items.correctChoice: Some students may have used the later hours for group study.

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2017 05 VN.pdf
items.prompt: A student claims that a transit app involving 834 participants had a measurable effect during a spring trial. Which finding, if true, would most directly support the claim?
items.correctChoice: After riders received real-time delay notices, missed transfers became less common.

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2017 05 VN.pdf
items.prompt:
During the archive note, after reviewing 47 field notes, the researcher's summary was unusually ______: it was supported by independent records and repeated observations.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word?
items.correctChoice: credible

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2017 05 VN.pdf
items.prompt:
During a March budget discussion with 70 planners, the text first describes a challenge associated with green roofs, especially installation costs. It then explains a possible benefit: cooler building interiors.

Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
items.correctChoice: It presents a drawback of an approach and then identifies a potential advantage of that approach.

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2017 05 VN.pdf
items.prompt:
During a field check after 34 days, the first trial suggested that the material could withstand high heat. ______ the second trial showed that it became brittle after repeated heating.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
items.correctChoice: However,

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2018 03 US.pdf
items.prompt:
A 2019 study of bird migration surveyed 876 participants and compared 127 sites that differed in seasonal timing. After tracking the sites for 3 months, the researchers found that tracking data showed that some birds adjusted routes after unusually warm springs. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of bird migration found that differences in seasonal timing were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.sourceReference: SAT/REAL TESTS/2018 03 US.pdf
items.prompt:
A 2023 study of ancient pottery surveyed 877 participants and compared 124 sites that differed in trade routes. After tracking the sites for 4 months, the researchers found that chemical traces showed that clay vessels moved between distant towns. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of ancient pottery found that differences in trade routes were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Princeton review /Crack the new Sat online practice/Cracking_SAT_2020_Prem_Online_Practice_Test_6.pdf
items.prompt:
A 2014 study of battery design surveyed 986 participants and compared 147 sites that differed in storage capacity. After tracking the sites for 3 months, the researchers found that a revised separator allowed cells to hold a charge for longer periods. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of battery design found that differences in storage capacity were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Princeton review /Crack the new Sat online practice/Cracking_SAT_2020_Prem_Online_Practice_Test_6.pdf
items.prompt:
A 2018 study of bird migration surveyed 987 participants and compared 144 sites that differed in seasonal timing. After tracking the sites for 4 months, the researchers found that tracking data showed that some birds adjusted routes after unusually warm springs. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of bird migration found that differences in seasonal timing were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Princeton review /Crack the new Sat online practice/Cracking_SAT_2020_Prem_Online_Practice_Test_6.pdf

After tracking the sites for 5 months, the researchers found that chemical traces showed that clay vessels moved between distant towns. They concluded that local conditions can shape outcomes in ways that broad regional averages may miss.

Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study of ancient pottery found that differences in trade routes were linked to meaningful local outcomes.

items.sourceReference: SAT/PRACTICE TESTS/Princeton review /Crack the new Sat online practice/Cracking_SAT_2020_Prem_Online_Practice_Test_6.pdf

items.prompt:
Text 1: Some historians argue that local newspapers mainly reflected elite viewpoints because owners controlled what was printed after a six-week pilot.
Text 2: Other historians note that letters to the editor sometimes allowed ordinary readers to challenge those viewpoints during the same period.

Which choice best describes how the author of Text 2 would most likely respond to the claim in Text 1?
items.correctChoice: By agreeing partly but adding that newspapers could also contain dissenting public voices.

items.correctChoice: By agreeing partly but adding that newspapers could also contain dissenting public voices.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
items.correctChoice: organizes

items.prompt: A research team studying coastal kelp measured nutrient levels at matched sites before and after a policy change. The team found that sites with higher nutrient levels also had improved canopy density, but the researchers warned that the study did not isolate every factor affecting nearby fish diversity. Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
items.correctChoice: A study found an association between one measured condition and an outcome, while noting a limit on causal interpretation.

items.prompt:
A scientist claims that increasing compost mixtures is associated with stronger nitrogen availability. The table summarizes four matched field sites.

Site A: compost mixtures index 18, nitrogen availability index 24
Site B: compost mixtures index 24, nitrogen availability index 31
Site C: compost mixtures index 31, nitrogen availability index 43
Site D: compost mixtures index 43, nitrogen availability index 51

Which finding from the table, if true, most directly supports the scientist's claim?
items.correctChoice: As the compost mixtures index rises across the four sites, the nitrogen availability index also rises.
